seoTitle: How to Create a Child Issue in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to add a child issue in Jira to break parent work items down into smaller, trackable pieces.
intro: Jira's newer hierarchy lets you attach child issues to a parent so large pieces of work can be split up without losing the connection between them. This guide walks through creating a child issue from an existing parent.

steps.heading: Open the parent issue
steps.body: Go to the issue that should act as the parent and open its detail view.

steps.heading: Locate the child issues section
steps.body: Scroll to the area of the issue where related child issues can be added or listed.

steps.heading: Create a new child issue
steps.body: Choose to add a child issue, give it a summary, and select the appropriate issue type.

steps.heading: Set details on the child issue
steps.body: Add an assignee, priority, and any other fields the child issue needs before saving it.

steps.heading: Track progress from the parent
steps.body: Use the parent issue's view to see the status of all its child issues as they move through the workflow.

faq.question: What's the difference between a child issue and a subtask?
faq.answer: Child issues are part of Jira's newer hierarchy and can be full issue types, while subtasks are a simpler, single-level breakdown.

faq.question: Can a child issue have its own child issues?
faq.answer: This depends on how your hierarchy levels are configured, since Jira supports multiple levels beyond the default.

faq.question: Do child issues appear on the board separately?
faq.answer: Yes, child issues typically appear as their own cards depending on your board's filter and layout settings.

faq.question: Can I remove the parent-child link later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can unlink a child issue from its parent at any time without deleting the issue itself.
